_comment_secret_scanning: Scan for secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords). Supported engines: gitleaks, betterleaks, leaktk, trufflehog, detect-secrets, secretlint, gitguardian

secret_scanning._comment_engines: Engines tried in order. Built-in: gitleaks, betterleaks, leaktk, trufflehog, detect-secrets, secretlint, gitguardian. Cloud engines (gitguardian) require consent: ai-guardian engine consent gitguardian
secret_scanning._comment_strategy: Strategies: first-match (default), any-match (block if ANY finds secrets), consensus (block only if N agree)
secret_scanning._comment_cache: Result caching: skip re-scanning unchanged content (v1.7.0+)
secret_scanning._comment_incremental: Incremental scanning: only scan files whose content changed (v1.7.0+, requires cache_results)
secret_scanning._comment_audit: Audit logging: log all scan operations for compliance (v1.7.0+)

_comment_prompt_injection: Detect and block prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior
_comment_secret_redaction: Redact secrets from tool outputs instead of blocking (NEW in v1.5.0, Phase 4)
_comment_scan_pii: PII detection for GDPR/CCPA compliance (NEW in v1.6.0). Strict profile: all PII types including email.
_comment_ssrf_protection: Prevent SSRF attacks by blocking access to private networks, metadata endpoints, and dangerous URL schemes (NEW in v1.5.0)

ssrf_protection._comment_path_based_rules: Path-based filtering for granular access control (NEW in v1.6.0) - Allow/block specific URL paths on domains

_comment_config_file_scanning: Detect credential exfiltration commands in AI config files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) - Phase 3 of Hermes integration (NEW in v1.5.0)
_comment_permissions: Control which tools (Skills, MCP servers, Bash, etc.) are allowed to run

rules._comment: Skills - Blocked by default. Add allow rules via Console.
MCP Servers - Blocked by default. Add allow rules via Console.

_comment_permissions_directories: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover tool permissions from directories/GitHub repos. Scans for permission files and merges discovered rules into permissions.rules. Most users should use remote_configs instead.

_permissions_directories_example._comment: Example: scan local skills directory to auto-allow discovered skills
Example: scan GitHub repository for skills

_comment_directory_rules: OPTIONAL: Control AI access to specific directories (e.g., block ~/.ssh). Last-match-wins evaluation order. See ai-guardian-example.json for examples.
_comment_remote_configs: Load additional policies from remote URLs (for enterprise/team policies)
_comment_transcript_scanning: Scan conversation transcript for secrets, PII, and prompt injection from ! shell commands (NEW in v1.7.0, Issue #430)
_comment_annotations: Inline annotation suppression for secrets and PII (NEW in v1.8.0, Issue #481). Hardcoded: ai-guardian:allow (inline), ai-guardian:begin-allow/end-allow (block). Configurable aliases: inline_allow (secrets+PII), inline_allow_secrets (secrets only). Prompt injection, jailbreak, config exfil always scanned. Set enabled to false for strict compliance environments.
_comment_violation_logging: Log blocked operations for audit and review (NEW in v1.1.0)
_comment_daemon: Background daemon for faster hook processing. Modes: 'auto' (default, daemon with fallback), 'local' (per-process, CI/CD), 'daemon' (require daemon, for testing). Override with AI_GUARDIAN_DAEMON_MODE env var.
_comment_on_scan_error: Global behavior when a scanner encounters an error. Strict profile: fail-closed (block on error).
